description.default:
## Code Readability & Maintainability

- Write self-documenting code with clear, descriptive variable and function names.
- Keep functions short and focused on a single responsibility.
- Avoid deeply nested conditionals — use early returns and guard clauses.
- Prefer composition over inheritance.
- Follow the DRY principle (Don't Repeat Yourself) but not at the cost of clarity.
- Use meaningful comments to explain **why**, not **what**.
